data.body:
Q3 — DESIGN: add an agent doing a NARROW prior-art search to screen for proposed patterns OSS peers have REJECTED (tried-and-abandoned, or never adopted because unnecessary). Expensive (web research). Want: run on architecturally-complex tickets, max once per ticket, ideally re-run on significant architecture revision (v2). Proposed shape: overlay triggered by T1 findings.

Architecture mapping (from reading orchestrator.py, registry.py, sizing.py, attest.py, pass1.py, det_floor.py, runner.py, pai_tools.py, config.py, and gates/plan-review.yaml + executor.py/interpreter.py):

1) TRIGGER (the crux). Inter-criterion finding-dependency DOES NOT EXIST at the criteria layer — route_criteria (orchestrator.py:123-153) selects criteria statically BEFORE any LLM runs; all finders run concurrently in one Pass-1 batch; T1 findings don't exist at selection time; no depends_on/triggered_by field in criteria_routing.json. HOWEVER the WORKFLOW ENGINE supports conditional execution: step 'if:' (executor.py:470-487) and 'branch.when' (interpreter.py:513-539), already used for run_llm and code_grounded branches in gates/plan-review.yaml. So two shapes:
   - Shape A (RECOMMENDED v1): finder-batch overlay like other T-overlays (criteria_routing.json entry + reviewer prompt + batch 'when: include_<ID>'). Inherits verify/decide/coach/checkpoint/signing free. Cannot gate on 'T1 FOUND something' — gate instead on T1's bright-lines + a complexity predicate. Runs whenever (bright-line AND complex) even if T1 clean; bounded by once-per-plan-version cache.
   - Shape B (defer to v2): conditional follow-up step after decide with if:(t1_surfaced && complex). Honors exact intent but bespoke: must expose t1_surfaced + complexity as step outputs and thread findings into coach; no free checkpoint.

2) BIGGEST GAP = web/OSS research tooling. Agentic criteria today get filesystem + rebar ticket tools + configured MCP toolsets only (pai_tools.py:42-207); NO web/GitHub search by default. T1's 'tool-grounded (web/codebase)' is codebase-only in practice. Must add a native web/GitHub-search tool to pai_tools.py (inject via runner extra_tools) OR wire a search MCP via REBAR_LLM_MCP_SERVERS. This is the real net-new work.

3) ONCE-PER-TICKET = mostly free. Checkpoint cache keyed on MATERIAL fingerprint (description+AC+file_impact+children) at .rebar/cache/plan-review/<ticket>/ (sizing.py:240-295); Shape A memoized automatically => once-per-plan-version, auto-invalidates on material edit (also gives crude 're-run on revision' free, but ANY material edit re-triggers, no significant-vs-trivial notion). CAVEAT: cache is LOCAL/git-ignored => once-per-CLONE, not global. For global once-only, persist result into the SIGNED attestation manifest (attest.py build_manifest) or a store event on the tickets branch.

4) COMPLEXITY GATE = reuse existing signals: G5 decomposition triggers (>3 files / >=3 layers / new interface), P4 oversize (>25 AC, >30 file_impact, >24k chars) det_floor.py:309-347, centrality (blast radius) sizing.py:49-57.

5) BUDGET/LATENCY = will bite. Overlay AGENT criteria are SHED FIRST under budget pressure (sizing.py:188-236; cost model prices AGENT at $0.12, web research is far more) => criterion gets dropped before running. Must: bump cost weight, EXEMPT from shed queue, raise REBAR_PLAN_REVIEW_BUDGET; likely a dedicated LLMConfig with higher max_iterations/timeout (defaults 25/600s, config.py:32-40). Latency: runs INLINE in the claim-blocking gate path — a multi-minute web search blocks whoever runs review-plan; consider out-of-band. Per-criterion model override doesn't exist (single base model + runtime size-ladder escalation, runner.py:382-394).

6) REGISTRATION boilerplate: reviewer prompt src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan-review-<ID>.md (execution_mode: agentic) + criteria_routing.json entry + register in CANONICAL_LLM + 'python -m rebar.llm.prompts regenerate-index' (CI drift gate enforces).

RECOMMENDED V1 CUT: Shape A, triggered on T1 bright-lines + complexity gate (not T1 findings); reuse material-fingerprint checkpoint; the only genuinely net-new pieces are the web/OSS-search tool and the budget-shedding exemption/cost-weight. Defer true finding-triggering (Shape B) and significant-vs-trivial revision detection to v2.

SIDE OBSERVATION (not investigated): rebar CLI warns 'unknown key verify.require_plan_review_for_claim ignored (typo?)' on every command against rebar.toml, yet CLAUDE.md says the plan-review claim gate is on for this project. Worth verifying the config key name is correct — the gate may be silently inactive.

No ticket created yet; offered to write this up as an epic/story with AC + v1/v2 split or prototype the web-research tool wiring first.
